dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4296 IS: IGO Anti-Boycott Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Scott of Florida (for himself, Mrs. Britt, Mr. Hagerty, Mr. Hoeven, Mr. Scott of South Carolina, Mr. Ricketts, and Mr. Budd) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations

form.official-title: To amend the Anti-Boycott Act of 2018 to apply the provisions of that Act to international governmental organizations.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the IGO Anti-Boycott Act.

section.header: Amendments to the Anti-Boycott Act of 2018
section.text: The Anti-Boycott Act of 2018 is amended—

paragraph.text: in section 1772 (50 U.S.C. 4841), by inserting or international governmental organization after foreign country each place it appears; and

clause.text: in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), by inserting or international governmental organization after foreign country;
in subparagraph (A), in the first sentence, by inserting or international governmental organization after boycotting country; and
in subparagraph (D), in the first sentence, by inserting or international governmental organization after boycotting country; and

subparagraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: The President shall submit to Congress and make available to the public on an annual basis a report that contains—

subparagraph.text: a list of those foreign countries and international governmental organizations that foster or impose boycotts and with respect to which this section applies; and
